title: BUG: segfault from calling `__func__` of Cython methods
author: devdanzin
body:
### Describe the issue:

It's possible to segfault the interpreter by calling `__func__()` of some Cython methods.

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
import numpy.random
numpy.random.poisson.__func__(list)
```

### Error message:

```shell
Thread 1 "python" received signal SIGSEGV, Segmentation fault.
find_name_in_mro (type=type@entry=0x55555585df76, name=name@entry=0x555555a5ff18 <_PyRuntime+48088>, error=error@entry=0x7fffffffcc14) at Objects/typeobject.c:5126
5126        Py_INCREF(mro);

#0  find_name_in_mro (type=type@entry=0x55555585df76, name=name@entry=0x555555a5ff18 <_PyRuntime+48088>, error=error@entry=0x7fffffffcc14) at Objects/typeobject.c:5126
#1  0x00005555556b7a12 in _PyType_LookupRef (type=0x55555585df76, name=0x555555a5ff18 <_PyRuntime+48088>) at Objects/typeobject.c:5284
#2  0x00005555556b9306 in _PyType_Lookup (type=0x55555585df76, name=0x555555a5ff18 <_PyRuntime+48088>) at Objects/typeobject.c:5321

   from /home/danzin/venvs/3.13_release_fusil_venv/lib/python3.13t/site-packages/numpy/random/_common.cpython-313t-x86_64-linux-gnu.so

   from /home/danzin/venvs/3.13_release_fusil_venv/lib/python3.13t/site-packages/numpy/random/_common.cpython-313t-x86_64-linux-gnu.so

   from /home/danzin/venvs/3.13_release_fusil_venv/lib/python3.13t/site-packages/numpy/random/mtrand.cpython-313t-x86_64-linux-gnu.so

    kwnames=<optimized out>) at ./Include/internal/pycore_call.h:168

#11 PyEval_EvalCode (co=co@entry=0x20c37351e00, globals=globals@entry=0x20c366a9440, locals=locals@entry=0x20c366a9440) at Python/ceval.c:604

    kwnames=<optimized out>) at ./Include/internal/pycore_call.h:168

    at Objects/call.c:327

#27 0x00005555557ea8df in pymain_run_module (modname=<optimized out>, set_argv0=set_argv0@entry=0) at Modules/main.c:349
#28 0x00005555557eb139 in pymain_run_stdin (config=config@entry=0x555555a71ac8 <_PyRuntime+120712>) at Modules/main.c:575
#29 0x00005555557ea5b5 in pymain_run_python (exitcode=0x7fffffffd69c) at Modules/main.c:699
#30 Py_RunMain () at Modules/main.c:775
#31 0x00005555557ea77a in pymain_main (args=args@entry=0x7fffffffd988) at Modules/main.c:805

```

### Python and NumPy Versions:

```
2.3.0.dev0+git20250415.e151f0d
3.13.3+ experimental free-threading build (heads/3.13:83cb89b941b, Apr 18 2025, 20:59:43) [Clang 19.1.7 (++20250114103253+cd708029e0b2-1exp120250114103309.40)]
```

### Runtime Environment:

```
[{'numpy_version': '2.3.0.dev0+git20250415.e151f0d',
'python': '3.13.3+ experimental free-threading build '
'(heads/3.13:83cb89b941b, Apr 18 2025, 20:59:43) [Clang 19.1.7 '
'(++20250114103253+cd708029e0b2-1exp120250114103309.40)]',
'uname': uname_result(system='Linux', node='beesknees', release='6.11.0-24-generic', version='https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/24-Ubuntu SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Fri Mar 14 18:13:56 UTC 2025', machine='x86_64')},
{'simd_extensions': {'baseline': ['SSE', 'SSE2', 'SSE3'],
'found': ['SSSE3',
'SSE41',
'POPCNT',
'SSE42',
'AVX',
'F16C',
'FMA3',
'AVX2',
'AVX512F',
'AVX512CD',
'AVX512_SKX',
'AVX512_CLX',
'AVX512_CNL',
'AVX512_ICL'],
'not_found': ['AVX512_KNL', 'AVX512_KNM', 'AVX512_SPR']}},
{'architecture': 'SkylakeX',
'filepath': '/home/danzin/venvs/3.13_upstream_fusil_venv/lib/python3.13t/site-packages/numpy.libs/libscipy_openblas64_-56d6093b.so',
'internal_api': 'openblas',
'num_threads': 16,
'prefix': 'libscipy_openblas',
'threading_layer': 'pthreads',
'user_api': 'blas',
'version': '0.3.29'}]
```

### Context for the issue:

I have been fuzzing Numpy using [fusil](https://github.com/devdanzin/fusil) by @vstinner. I realize these crashes are unlikely to be triggered in normal usage and therefore might be of low priority.

The fuzzing was done with an ASAN free-threading clang build and not confirmed on a GILfull non-sanitizer GCC build yet.

comments.author: seberg
comments.body:
Can you move this issue to Cython?  I thought, there was an issue about this in Cython already but didn't find it very quickly (I remember running into what I think is the same in a very different context).
(Might be good to double check it's Cython, but I am certain enough.  It will probably be related to using `#cython: binding=True`)

(Not saying that this is a big bug in Cython.)

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body: Will do, thanks for the feedback!
